_comment: Sample ClinicalTrials.gov v2 /studies response used by scripts/run_demo.py --mock. Three synthetic breast-cancer trials chosen to exercise the parser (inclusion+exclusion headers, locations, phases, sponsors). NCT IDs are placeholders.

identificationModule.briefTitle: Adjuvant Tamoxifen vs. Aromatase Inhibitor in Post-Menopausal Early Breast Cancer
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Randomized Phase 3 Trial of Adjuvant Tamoxifen Compared With Letrozole in Post-Menopausal Women With Hormone Receptor Positive Early-Stage Breast Cancer

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Female, age >= 18
- Histologically confirmed hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer
- ECOG performance status 0-2
- Post-menopausal status

Exclusion Criteria:
- Prior tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitor therapy
- Active second malignancy
- Severe hepatic impairment

identificationModule.briefTitle: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan in HER2-Low Metastatic Breast Cancer
identificationModule.officialTitle: An Open-Label Phase 2 Study of Trastuzumab Deruxtecan in Previously Treated HER2-Low Metastatic Breast Cancer

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Adults age 18-75
- HER2-low metastatic breast cancer (IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH-negative)
- At least one prior line of chemotherapy in the metastatic setting
- ECOG 0-1

Exclusion Criteria:
- History of interstitial lung disease
- Active brain metastases
- LVEF < 50%

identificationModule.briefTitle: Neoadjuvant Pembrolizumab Plus Chemotherapy in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer
identificationModule.officialTitle: Phase 3 Randomized Study of Neoadjuvant Pembrolizumab Plus Chemotherapy Versus Chemotherapy Alone in Early-Stage Triple-Negative Breast Cancer

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Female, age >= 18
- Histologically confirmed triple-negative breast cancer
- Clinical stage II-III
- ECOG 0-1
- Adequate organ and bone marrow function

Exclusion Criteria:
- Prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy for breast cancer
- Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy
- Known HIV, active hepatitis B, or hepatitis C
